item.text: The level is another vessel/stamp configuration: actions 3/4 roll the central object, action 5 applies its current face to the 10×10 work area, while 1/2 are blocked in the initial position. I’m reducing the frame to selector patterns, vessel position, and work-area fill counts, then testing short contextual sequences.

The target is now explicit: a 10×10 picture built from a black base plus cyan rectangle, yellow southeast region, and blue southwest region. I’ll enumerate the small set of reachable vessel placements and record each placement’s exact stamp mask, using pixel mismatch to the target as the dense progress measure.

item.text: A key mechanic is confirmed: stamps overwrite earlier paint. The 10×10 upper-left graphic cannot be composed from the eight observed footprint masks as a flat target, so I’m checking the earlier solved levels to distinguish “target picture” from vessel-state preview before planning further.

Earlier levels confirm the upper-left image is the exact work target. The missing ingredient is stateful rolling: a vessel face can carry prior paint, so flat-mask composition alone is insufficient. I’m switching to a bounded macro search over the eight physical placements and seven selector colors, scored by exact work-target mismatch and checked against real level completion.

The dense search reached 88/100 exact pixels. Its four-operation suffix paints every yellow/blue/black region correctly; the only mismatch is the 12-cell cyan rectangle. That strongly isolates the remaining subgoal: prepaint cyan at one vessel placement, return to the canonical north placement, then run the verified suffix. I’m testing only those eight bounded variants now.

item.text: The insertion tests show cyan cannot be added as an independent flat stamp; it must survive through a different rolling/painting sequence. I’m broadening the bounded beam slightly and preserving candidates that make verified progress on each target color separately, so preparatory cyan states are not discarded by total mismatch alone.
